entries.title: ENIAC unveiled
entries.description: The first general-purpose electronic digital computer was publicly demonstrated in Philadelphia.

entries.title: UNIVAC I delivered
entries.description: The first commercially produced computer in the United States was delivered to the US Census Bureau.

entries.title: Spacewar! released
entries.description: Widely regarded as the first widely-shared digital video game, created for lab computers by MIT students.

entries.title: First email sent
entries.description: Ray Tomlinson sent the first network email between two computers on ARPANET, establishing the 'user@host' address format.

entries.title: Intel 4004 released
entries.description: The first commercially available single-chip microprocessor went on sale.

entries.title: Altair 8800 launched
entries.description: The first commercially successful personal computer kit sparked the home-computing hobbyist movement.

entries.title: First website goes live
entries.description: Tim Berners-Lee published info.cern.ch, the first website, at CERN.

entries.title: IBM Simon released
entries.description: The first device widely recognized as a smartphone combined a mobile phone with calendar, email, and touchscreen apps.

entries.title: First tweet posted
entries.description: Twitter co-founder Jack Dorsey posted the platform's first message: 'just setting up my twttr'.

entries.title: First iPhone unveiled
entries.description: Steve Jobs introduced the iPhone, merging a phone, iPod, and internet device into one touchscreen product.

entries.title: ChatGPT launches publicly
entries.description: OpenAI's public release of ChatGPT brought conversational AI to mainstream use almost overnight.
